tr.th: Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us

tr.td: The patient shows elevated serum creatinine, suggesting impaired renal function, and abnormal urine findings including proteinuria, glucosuria, ketonuria, and bilirubinuria, with low urine specific gravity. These findings indicate possible renal dysfunction, tubular damage, or acute metabolic disturbance. Low HbA1c suggests no chronic hyperglycemia. Urine color and appearance may indicate underlying pathology. Clinical correlation and further nephrological and metabolic evaluation are recommended.

display.value: Microalbuminuria due to type 2 diabetes mellitus
Disorder of kidney due to diabetes mellitus
